perturbation.original_text: The term "Confidential Information" as used in this Agreement shall mean any data or information that is competitively sensitive material and not generally known to the public, including, but not limited to, information relating to any of the following: business plans, design, improvements, marketing strategies, present or future business activities, product development and plans, data, databases, documentation, flow charts, formulas, reports, specifications, finance, operations, procedures, processes, supplier lists, supplier profiles, supplier relationships, systems, computer software, inventions, know-how, object code, proprietary concepts, source code, technical information, trade secrets, customer lists, customer profiles, customer relationships, performance results, pricing, sales estimates, which the Disclosing Party considers confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Private Details" as used in this Document shall mean any data or information that is competitively sensitive material and not generally known to the public, including, but not limited to, information relating to any of the following: business plans, design, improvements, marketing strategies, present or future business activities, product development and plans, data, databases, documentation, flow charts, formulas, reports, specifications, finance, operations, procedures, processes, supplier lists, supplier profiles, supplier relationships, systems, computer software, inventions, know-how, object code, proprietary concepts, source code, technical information, trade secrets, customer lists, customer profiles, customer relationships, performance results, pricing, sales estimates, which the Disclosing Party considers confidential.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Details' while the remainder of the agreement still refers to 'Confidential Information' creates ambiguity. The obligations regarding confidentiality become unclear because the defined term doesn't match the term used throughout the contract. The agreement references 'Confidential Information' and that is not being defined anywhere else within the text.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Details' directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which repeatedly refers to 'Confidential Information.' The changed text defines 'Private Details' but does not define 'Confidential Information,' rendering the obligations in the contradicted text unclear and unenforceable because the agreement references 'Confidential Information' which is undefined, thus a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the termination of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will ensure that all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings or electronic records prepared by it that include or reflect any Confidential Information are returned or destroyed as directed by the Disclosing Party
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the termination of this Contract, the Information Receiver will ensure that all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings or electronic records prepared by it that include or reflect any Secret Data are returned or destroyed as directed by the Data Source
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Agreement', 'Receiving Party', 'Confidential Information', and 'Disclosing Party' are respectively replaced with 'Contract', 'Information Receiver', 'Secret Data', and 'Data Source'. The earlier part of the agreement uses the original terms, so it will be inconsistent to replace those terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Non-Disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as of January 01, 2018 by and between Kennetik, a Minnesota Limited Liability Company ("Kennetik") and , as an Individual (" "). Kennetik and have indicated an interest in exploring a potential business relationship relating to: The hiring of Kennetik to perform content development & strategy, including but not limited to conceptualizing, producing and managing the client's content. (the "Transaction"). In connection with the parties' respective evaluation of the Transaction, Kennetik, its respective affiliates and its respective directors, officers, employees, agents or advisors (collectively, "Representatives") may provide or grant access to certain confidential and proprietary information to . The party disclosing its Confidential Information (as defined herein) to the other party is hereafter referred to as the "Disclosing Party." The party receiving the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is hereafter referred to as the "Receiving Party."
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces key terms like 'Agreement', 'Receiving Party', 'Confidential Information', and 'Disclosing Party' with 'Contract', 'Information Receiver', 'Secret Data', and 'Data Source' respectively. The contradicted text defines 'Agreement', 'Receiving Party', and 'Disclosing Party' with specific meanings within the context of 'Confidential Information'. Since the changed text uses entirely different terms to describe the same entities and subject matter, it creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text relies on the original terms to establish the relationship and obligations of the parties. The misaligned terminology leads to an in-text contradiction as the document now uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations as defined in the perturbation definitions.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that use or disclosure of any Confidential Information in a manner inconsistent with this Agreement will give rise to irreparable injury for which: (a) money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by such party; (b) the other party may be entitled to specific performance and injunction and other equitable relief with respect to any such breach; (c) such remedies will not be the exclusive remedies for any such breach, but will be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity; and (d) in the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines in a final non-appealable order that one party, or any of its Representatives, has breached this Agreement, such party will be liable for reasonable legal fees and expenses incurred by the other party in connection with such litigation, including, but not limited to, any appeals.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that use or disclosure of any Private Data in a manner inconsistent with this Document will give rise to irreparable injury for which: (a) money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Document by such party; (b) the other party may be entitled to specific performance and injunction and other equitable relief with respect to any such breach; (c) such remedies will not be the exclusive remedies for any such breach, but will be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity; and (d) in the event of litigation relating to this Document, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines in a final non-appealable order that one party, or any of its Representatives, has breached this Document, such party will be liable for reasonable legal fees and expenses incurred by the other party in connection with such litigation, including, but not limited to, any appeals.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Private Data' and 'Agreement' with 'Document' in the 'Remedies' section, while these terms are inconsistently used throughout the text, generates internal contradiction. The rest of the document still uses the original terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Non-Disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as of January 01, 2018 by and between Kennetik, a Minnesota Limited Liability Company ("Kennetik") and , as an Individual (" "). Kennetik and have indicated an interest in exploring a potential business relationship relating to: The hiring of Kennetik to perform content development & strategy, including but not limited to conceptualizing, producing and managing the client's content. (the "Transaction"). In connection with the parties' respective evaluation of the Transaction, Kennetik, its respective affiliates and its respective directors, officers, employees, agents or advisors (collectively, "Representatives") may provide or grant access to certain confidential and proprietary information to . The party disclosing its Confidential Information (as defined herein) to the other party is hereafter referred to as the "Disclosing Party." The party receiving the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is hereafter referred to as the "Receiving Party."
perturbation.justification: The original text references 'Confidential Information' and 'Agreement'. The changed text replaces these terms with 'Private Data' and 'Document' respectively. The 'Contradicted Text' section defines 'Agreement' and uses 'Confidential Information' without ever defining 'Private Data' or 'Document'. Because the changed text uses different terminology that is inconsistently applied throughout the document (as stated in the perturbation explanation), this generates internal contradiction and falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will terminate on the earlier of: (a) the written agreement of the parties to terminate this Agreement; (b) the consummation of the Transaction; or (c) 5 years from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Arrangement will terminate on the earlier of: (a) the written agreement of the parties to terminate this Arrangement; (b) the consummation of the Transaction; or (c) 5 years from the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'Arrangement' creates inconsistency as the document primarily refers to itself as the 'Agreement.' This will cause confusion about the enforceability and scope of the termination clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Non-Disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as of January 01, 2018 by and between Kennetik, a Minnesota Limited Liability Company ("Kennetik") and , as an Individual (" "). Kennetik and have indicated an interest in exploring a potential business relationship relating to: The hiring of Kennetik to perform content development & strategy, including but not limited to conceptualizing, producing and managing the client's content. (the "Transaction"). In connection with the parties' respective evaluation of the Transaction, Kennetik, its respective affiliates and its respective directors, officers, employees, agents or advisors (collectively, "Representatives") may provide or grant access to certain confidential and proprietary information to . The party disclosing its Confidential Information (as defined herein) to the other party is hereafter referred to as the "Disclosing Party." The party receiving the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is hereafter referred to as the "Receiving Party."
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Agreement,' which is the defined term in the 'Contradicted Text.' The changed text replaces 'Agreement' with 'Arrangement.' This misaligned terminology creates an in-text contradiction because the contract consistently refers to itself as the 'Agreement,' as explicitly stated in the definition of the 'Contradicted Text,' which defines 'Agreement' in the first sentence: 'This Non-Disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement (this "Agreement").' Using 'Arrangement' instead of 'Agreement' introduces ambiguity and conflicts with the established terminology, creating uncertainty about which contract the termination clause applies to. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
